zh.Combe Raleigh, Devon, England: 英國戴文可姆 雷利
zh.Cheadle, Staffordshire, England: 英國英格蘭斯塔福爾黑阿德爾

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Ralph Le Brett於1200年出生在英國艾可時，他的父親Ralph le Brett I30歲，母親Alice de Coleville30歲。他於大約1252年與Mrs- Ralph Le Brett在英國英格蘭艾可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1252年在英國爾黑阿德爾享年52歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Ralph Le Brett于1200年出生在英国艾可时，他的父亲Ralph le Brett I30岁，母亲Alice de Coleville30岁。他于大约1252年与Mrs- Ralph Le Brett在英国英格兰艾可结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1252年在英国爾黑阿德爾享年52岁。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Brett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: (i) ethnic name, usually from Old French Bret, nominative of Breton ‘Breton’. (ii) ethnic name from Middle English Brit, Bret, Brut (Old English Brit, Bret, Bryt) ‘a Briton’, applied to the Strathclyde Britons up till the 13th century.

variants.name: Bret
meanings: French: ethnic name for a Breton, from Old French bret, nominative of breton ‘Breton’ (see Breton ). See also English Brett .

variants.name: Britto
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Alvaro, Dominga, Eufemia, Fermino, Josue, Juana, Pedro, Romulo, Serafin.
Portuguese: variant of   Brito  . This surname is also found in western India, where it was taken by Portuguese colonists.
English: ethnic name from medieval Latin Brito ‘Briton or Breton’, translating Norman French (Le) Bret (compare Brett ).

variants.name: Bratt
meanings: English (West Midlands): from Middle English brat ‘cloak of coarse cloth’ (Old English bratt), possibly denoting a maker or habitual wearer of such garments. Alternatively, perhaps a variant of Brett .
Swedish and Norwegian: from the Old Norse personal name Brattr meaning ‘majestic, proud’ (also, of places, ‘steep’). See also Bradt .

variants.name: Breton
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Normand, Renald, Adelard, Alphonse, Armand, Gaetan, Laurent, Serge, Adrien, Aime.
French, Breton (mainly Finistère), and English (of Norman origin): ethnic name for a Breton, from Old French breton, oblique case of bret (see Bret ). Compare Lebreton , see also English Brett .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne by Jean Élie/Hélie dit (Le) Breton from Ménéac in Morbihan, Brittany (France), who married Jeanne Labbé in Sainte-Famille, Île d'Orléans, QC, in 1669. His descendants also bear the surname Elie .

variants.name: Britts
meanings: Probably an Americanized form of German   Britz  .
English: variant of Britt (see Brett ), with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Britt
meanings: English: ethnic name for a Celtic-speaking Briton or a Breton, from Middle English brit, bret, Old French bret (see Brett ).
German: from a vernacular form of the personal name Brixius (see Brice ).

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Ralph Le Brett was born in 1200, in Essex, England, his father, Ralph le Brett I, was 30 and his mother, Alice de Coleville, was 30. He married Mrs- Ralph Le Brett about 1252, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1252, in Cheadle, Cheshire, England, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Ralph Le Brett est né en 1200, à Essex, Angleterre, son père, Ralph le Brett I, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Alice de Coleville, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Mrs- Ralph Le Brett environ 1252, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1252, à Cheadle, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Ralph Le Brett 1200 in Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ralph le Brett I 30 und seine Mutter Alice de Coleville 30. Er hat um 1252 in Essex, England, United Kingdom Mrs- Ralph Le Brett geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1252 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Cheadle, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Ralph Le Brett è nato il nel 1200 nel Essex, England, il suo padre, Ralph le Brett I, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Alice de Coleville, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Mrs- Ralph Le Brett circa 1252, nel Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1252, nel Cheadle, Cheshire, England, all'età di 52 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Ralph Le Brettが1200年にイギリスエセックスで生まれた時，父Ralph le Brett Iは30歳，母Alice de Colevilleは30歳であった。約1252年にイギリスイギリスエセックスでMrs- Ralph Le Brettと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1252年にイギリスチェシュアイアチェアドルで52歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Ralph Le Brett(이)가 1200년 영국 에세스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph le Brett I(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Coleville(은)는 30세였다. 그는 대략 1252년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 Mrs- Ralph Le Brett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1252년 영국 체실르 체아들일에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Ralph Le Brett nasceu em 1200, em Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ralph le Brett I, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Coleville, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Mrs- Ralph Le Brett aproximadamente 1252, em Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1252, em Cheadle, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 52 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Ralph Le Brett родился в 1200 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Ralph le Brett I было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Coleville, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs- Ralph Le Brett около 1252 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1252 г. в местности чидл, чешир, Англия в возрасте 52 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Ralph Le Brett nació en 1200, en Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Ralph le Brett I, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Alice de Coleville, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Mrs- Ralph Le Brett aproximadamente 1252, en Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1252, en Cheadle, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 52 años.
